claim: Antibiotic induced alterations in the gut microbiome reduce resistance against Clostridium difficile
evidence_sentences: Antibiotics can have significant and long-lasting effects on the gastrointestinal tract microbiota, reducing colonization resistance against pathogens including Clostridium difficile.
Our results indicate that antibiotic-mediated alteration of the gut microbiome converts the global metabolic profile to one that favours C. difficile germination and growth.
